name: Labor Day
description: The first Monday of September — the unofficial end of summer and a celebration of the U.S. labor movement.

questions.q: On what day of the week is U.S. Labor Day always observed?
In what year did Labor Day become a U.S. federal holiday?

source.notes: Signed by President Grover Cleveland on June 28, 1894.

questions.q: Which U.S. president signed Labor Day into law as a federal holiday?

source.notes: Six days after troops broke the Pullman Strike.

questions.q: Where was the first Labor Day parade held in 1882?

source.notes: Sponsored by the Central Labor Union, held September 5, 1882.

questions.q: Which U.S. state was the first to officially recognize Labor Day as a public holiday?

source.notes: Made it a state holiday in 1887.

questions.q: Which 1894 nationwide rail strike spurred the federal Labor Day legislation?

source.notes: Led by Eugene Debs's American Railway Union.

questions.q: Why did Cleveland choose September instead of May 1 for Labor Day?
choices: To distance from socialist/anarchist May Day

source.original_a: It might strengthen socialist and anarchist movements
source.notes: May 1 was associated with the 1886 Haymarket Affair in Chicago.

questions.q: Which May date is celebrated worldwide as International Workers' Day?
Which 1886 Chicago labor incident inspired International Workers' Day?

source.notes: May 4, 1886 bomb attack at a labor rally.

questions.q: Which is widely considered the unofficial end of summer in the U.S.?

source.notes: Memorial Day = unofficial start; Labor Day = unofficial end.

questions.q: Which traditional summer fashion 'rule' is supposed to be retired after Labor Day?

source.notes: 'No white after Labor Day' is a fading 19th-century elite fashion convention.

questions.q: Which charity telethon was a Labor Day weekend tradition for over 40 years until 2010?

source.notes: Hosted by Jerry Lewis from 1966-2010 for the Muscular Dystrophy Association.

questions.q: Which annual college football week is commonly known as 'Labor Day Weekend'?

source.notes: Most teams open their season the Saturday of Labor Day weekend.

questions.q: Which annual independent-arts festival is held on Labor Day weekend in the Nevada desert?

source.notes: Black Rock Desert; runs the week leading up to Labor Day.

questions.q: Who was the union leader-turned-Socialist Party candidate who led the 1894 Pullman Strike?

source.notes: Debs was jailed during the strike; later ran for president 5 times.

questions.q: Which carpenter is credited as the 'Father of Labor Day' for proposing it in 1882?

source.notes: Co-founded the American Federation of Labor (AFL). Some historians credit machinist Matthew Maguire instead.

questions.q: Approximately how many U.S. workers belong to a labor union today (as percentage)?
Which 1935 law guaranteed most U.S. workers the right to form unions and collectively bargain?
choices: Wagner Act / National Labor Relations Act

source.notes: Signed by FDR; established the NLRB.

questions.q: Canada also celebrates Labor Day on the same day. What month is it called 'Fête du Travail' in Quebec?

source.notes: Canada also adopted the first Monday of September in 1894.

questions.q: Which sport's regular season traditionally begins the week of Labor Day in the U.S.?

source.notes: College on Labor Day weekend; NFL the Thursday after.
